title: Incidence and prevalence of extensively drug-resistant tuberculosis
subtitle: The estimated number of people living with [extensively drug-resistant tuberculosis](#dod:drug-resistant-tuberculosis) (prevalence) versus new cases each year (incidence).
caption:
Incidence and prevalence of extensively drug-resistant tuberculosis, World

The estimated number of people living with extensively drug-resistant tuberculosis (prevalence) versus new cases each year (incidence).

This line chart tracks the global estimated number of people living with extensively drug-resistant tuberculosis compared to the number of new cases occurring annually between 1990 and 2023. The vertical axis measures the count of individuals ranging from 0 to 35,000, while the horizontal axis marks time in five-year increments. Two distinct trend lines appear: a blue line representing prevalence and an orange line representing incidence. Both metrics begin near zero in 1990 and follow a similar upward trajectory through the mid-1990s. From 1995 to 2010, the blue line for prevalence rises steeply, climbing from roughly 3,000 to approximately 28,000. During this same period, the orange line for incidence also increases but at a slower rate, reaching about 21,000 by 2010. After 2010, the slope of the incidence line flattens considerably, showing a gradual rise to just under 25,000 by 2023. In contrast, the prevalence line continues to ascend more steadily, reaching roughly 33,000 by the end of the timeline. The data includes tuberculosis cases in HIV-negative people, while tuberculosis cases among people with HIV are classified under HIV/AIDS cases. The gap between the two lines widens significantly after 2000, showing that the total number of people living with the condition grows faster than the rate of new infections. By 2023, the prevalence figure stands approximately 8,000 to 9,000 cases higher than the annual incidence figure.
